2604.24293.title: Latent-Hysteresis Graph ODEs: Modeling Coupled Topology-Feature Evolution via Continuous Phase Transitions
2604.24293.paper_summary: Graph neural ordinary differential equations (Graph ODEs) extend graph learning from discrete message-passing layers to continuous-time representation flows. While it supports adaptive long-range propagation, we show that Graph ODEs with strictly positive irreducible mixing operators face an inherent \emph{monostability trap}: in the long-time regime, information leakage is unavoidable and the dynamics converge to a single global consensus attractor. We propose the \textbf{Hysteresis Graph ODE (HGODE)}, which couples feature evolution with a latent topological potential driven by a learned pairwise force. A double-well edge potential and bipolarized gate allow edge states to polarize into connected or insulated phases while preserving differentiability. We provide asymptotic analysis of the collapse mechanism and the proposed hysteretic topology dynamics, and validate HGODE on theory-driven synthetic diagnostics and real-world graph benchmarks.
2604.24293.paper_summary_zh: 圖神經常微分方程（Graph ODEs）將圖學習從離散的訊息傳遞層擴展到連續時間的表示流。<lb/>雖然它支持自適應的長距離傳播，但我們顯示具有嚴格正的不可約混合運算子的 Graph ODEs 面臨固有的 \emph{單穩態陷阱}：在長時間範圍內，信息泄漏是不可避免的，動態收斂到單一的全局共識吸引子。<lb/>我們提出了 \textbf{滯後圖神經常微分方程（HGODE）}，它將特徵演化與由學習的成對力驅動的潛在拓撲勢能耦合。<lb/>雙井邊緣勢能和雙極化閘允許邊緣狀態極化為連接或絕緣相，同時保持可微性。<lb/>我們提供了崩潰機制和提出的滯後拓撲動力學的漸進分析，並在理論驅動的合成診斷和現實世界的圖基準上驗證 HGODE。
2604.24293.author: Qinhan Hou et.al.
